items.html: It follows that the discernment of the Malchut in the female is not a complete discernment like the other nine Sefirot in her, which are complete discernments, from the Keter to the Yesod in her, exactly after the likeness of the male. The Malchut in her is only a slight, minimal illumination, alluded to in that thick, protruding flesh that stands there, as mentioned. The reason is that the whole discernment of the female is called Malchut; hence, since her entire self is Malchut, there is no need for a discernment of Malchut to be in her, and therefore the Malchut in her was not a complete discernment, as mentioned. Nevertheless, a discernment of Malchut did need to be there, though only by way of allusion, as mentioned; all this was for a great need, and it is alluded to in the words of our sages, in their saying that four things require a seal within a seal, and their sign is ChaVIT: Chalav [milk], Basar [meat], Yayin [wine], Techelet [azure].
